proven.claim: Same early-return guard as line 298 — line 306 is only reachable if changes is non-empty
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 306
; Same early-return guard as line 298 — line 306 is only reachable if changes is non-empty

(declare-const changes_length Int)
(declare-const returned_at_275 Int)
(declare-const line_306_executes Int)

; Transition: early return fires when changes_length == 0
(assert (>= changes_length 0))
(assert (=> (= changes_length 0) (= returned_at_275 1)))
(assert (=> (= line_306_executes 1) (= returned_at_275 0)))

; Attempt to falsify: summary log fires with empty changes
(assert (= line_306_executes 1))
(assert (= changes_length 0))
(check-sat)

violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 306
; Summary: "${added} new | ${fixed} fixed | ${regressions} regressions | ${removed} removed"
; counts only 4 of 5 types — "unchanged" entries appear in loop output but not in totals
; When all changes are "unchanged": "0 new | 0 fixed | 0 regressions | 0 removed"
; while changes.length > 0 — semantically misleading summary

(declare-const changes_length Int)
(declare-const count_added Int)
(declare-const count_fixed Int)
(declare-const count_regressions Int)
(declare-const count_removed Int)
(declare-const count_unchanged Int)
(declare-const summary_total Int)

; Transition: each filter(c => c.type === X).length partitions changes_length across 5 types
(assert (>= changes_length 1))
(assert (>= count_added 0))
(assert (>= count_fixed 0))
(assert (>= count_regressions 0))
(assert (>= count_removed 0))
(assert (>= count_unchanged 0))
(assert (= changes_length
           (+ count_added (+ count_fixed (+ count_regressions (+ count_removed count_unchanged))))))

; Transition: summary_total is sum of only the 4 counted buckets
(assert (= summary_total (+ count_added (+ count_fixed (+ count_regressions count_removed)))))

; Violation: at least one "unchanged" entry → summary_total < changes_length
(assert (> count_unchanged 0))
(assert (< summary_total changes_length))
(check-sat)

clause_history.clause:

; LINE: 306
; Same early-return guard as line 298 — line 306 is only reachable if changes is non-empty

(declare-const changes_length Int)
(declare-const returned_at_275 Int)
(declare-const line_306_executes Int)

; Transition: early return fires when changes_length == 0
(assert (>= changes_length 0))
(assert (=> (= changes_length 0) (= returned_at_275 1)))
(assert (=> (= line_306_executes 1) (= returned_at_275 0)))

; Attempt to falsify: summary log fires with empty changes
(assert (= line_306_executes 1))
(assert (= changes_length 0))
(check-sat)
